skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: Use this skill when the user wants to produce a short video (5-120 seconds). Supports any video type: product ads, TikTok/Instagram/YouTube content, brand videos, explainers, social clips. USE FOR: video production, AI video, make a video, product video, brand video, promotional clip, explainer video, short video.
skill.summary: AI video creation agent that produces short videos (5-120s) for social media, ads, and brand content through the Pexo platform.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected external commands, network, filesystem, and environment variable access across 13 files (2110 lines). After evaluation all findings are confirmed false positives for malicious intent. The skill legitimately uses shell scripts to call the Pexo video API, reads its own PEXO_API_KEY from environment variables, and accesses ~/.pexo/ for configuration and temp files. No obfuscation, data exfiltration, command injection, or prompt injection was found. Risk is MEDIUM because the skill requires elevated privileges (env access, network calls, shell execution) but these are used solely for legitimate video creation functionality.

medium_findings.title: Shell script execution via command substitution
medium_findings.description: Shell scripts use command substitution ($(...) and backticks) throughout the codebase. This is legitimate for a CLI tool but represents a risk if script inputs are not validated. All script commands use hardcoded arguments or validated inputs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Static pattern confirmed. All instances use hardcoded curl/jq commands with no untrusted input injection vectors.

medium_findings.title: Environment variable access for API authentication
medium_findings.description: The skill reads PEXO_API_KEY and PEXO_BASE_URL from environment variables. This is required for the skill to authenticate with the Pexo API. The API key is used only in Authorization headers for Pexo API calls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Static pattern confirmed. PEXO_API_KEY is the skill's own documented requirement, used for legitimate API authentication to pexo.ai.

medium_findings.title: Network calls to external API
medium_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to pexo.ai for video creation, asset management, and status polling. All network destinations are the legitimate Pexo API. No unknown or suspicious endpoints detected.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Static pattern confirmed. All network destinations point to pexo.ai domain which matches the skill's documented homepage and API.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem access to user home directory
medium_findings.description: The skill reads from and writes to ~/.pexo/config and ~/.pexo/tmp/. This is the skill's own configuration directory used for storing API credentials and temporary video assets. Access is restricted to the .pexo subdirectory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Static pattern confirmed. Filesystem access is limited to the skill's own ~/.pexo/ directory for config and temp video storage.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in documentation files
low_findings.description: Documentation files (SKILL.md, SETUP-CHECKLIST.md, TROUBLESHOOTING.md) contain hardcoded URLs pointing to pexo.ai. These are example URLs and documentation references, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to pexo.ai domain in documentation context. These are instructional references, not executable network calls.

low_findings.title: Ruby backtick execution patterns in markdown code blocks
low_findings.description: Markdown files contain backtick characters within code blocks that match Ruby shell execution patterns. These are example bash commands in documentation code fences, not executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backtick patterns in markdown are inside documentation code blocks showing example bash commands. Not executable code.

low_findings.title: Standard device file access for output suppression
low_findings.description: Scripts redirect output to /dev/null for suppressing curl progress output and jq error messages. This is standard bash practice and not a security concern.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: /dev/null redirection is standard shell practice for suppressing output. No data leakage risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Combination of code execution, network access, and credential usage
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines shell script execution, HTTP requests to pexo.ai, and environment-based API key access. While this matches the pattern of a credential-exfiltrating trojan, the evaluation confirms this is expected behavior for a legitimate API client. All network destinations point to the skill's own documented API endpoints, and credentials are the skill's own API key used for authentication.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Pattern strongly matches credential-exfiltration heuristic, but semantic evaluation confirms legitimate intent. API key is the skill's own, used only for authenticating to the pexo.ai API.

content.user_title: Create Short AI Videos with Pexo Agent
content.value_statement: Producing short AI videos takes too long and requires technical video editing skills. This skill connects you to Pexo's AI video platform so you can create professional videos just by describing what you want.
actual_capabilities: Creates short AI-generated videos between 5 and 120 seconds
Supports three aspect ratios: 16:9 landscape, 9:16 portrait, and 1:1 square
Uploads image, video, and audio files as reference material for video generation
Manages project lifecycle: create project, submit request, poll for progress, and deliver final video
Handles credit checking and top-up flow when account credits run low
Supports video revision by sending new feedback to the same project
limitations: Requires a PEXO_API_KEY environment variable to authenticate with the Pexo platform
Video production takes approximately 15 to 20 minutes for a standard short video
Cannot create videos longer than 120 seconds in duration
Requires an active internet connection to communicate with the Pexo API

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Creation
use_cases.description: A content creator describes a TikTok or Instagram Reel concept and the skill handles the full video production workflow from idea to downloadable video file.
use_cases.target_user: Social media content creators and influencers

use_cases.title: Product Marketing Videos
use_cases.description: A marketer uploads product images and describes their vision for a promotional video. The skill creates the video using the uploaded assets as reference.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing professionals and small business owners

use_cases.title: Educational Explainer Videos
use_cases.description: An educator or trainer describes a concept they want to explain visually. The skill produces a short animated explainer video ready for sharing.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Video Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a short video about [topic]. Keep it under 30 seconds.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user wanting a simple video without any reference files

prompt_templates.title: Video With Reference Assets
prompt_templates.prompt: [Upload product photos] Create a promotional video using these images as reference. The video should be 15 seconds long in portrait format.
prompt_templates.scenario: User who has reference images or videos to include in their project

prompt_templates.title: Video Revision Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Change the music in the video to something more energetic and shorten the intro. Keep the same visuals.
prompt_templates.scenario: User who wants to modify an existing video project

prompt_templates.title: Full Workflow With Multiple Assets
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 60 second brand video in 16:9 format. I have an intro clip and a logo image to include. The video should explain our new product features in a professional tone.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user providing multiple reference files and specific creative requirements

output_examples.input: Create a 15 second product video for my new coffee shop. Make it warm and inviting.
output_examples.output: The skill creates a project, submits the request to Pexo, and after about 15 minutes returns a downloadable MP4 video URL showing a professionally edited coffee shop promotional video with transitions and background music.

output_examples.input: [Uploads photo of a running shoe] Make a 30 second Instagram Reel using this shoe photo. Show it in action.
output_examples.output: The skill uploads the shoe image as a reference asset, sends the creative brief to Pexo, and delivers a 30-second portrait-format video showing the shoe in various dynamic scenes with text overlays.

best_practices: Copy the user's request exactly as written when sending it to Pexo. Adding your own creative details reduces video quality.
Wait at least 60 seconds between each status poll to avoid triggering duplicate video production.
Send the full video download URL as plain text including all query parameters so the link works correctly.
anti_patterns: Adding duration, style, or aspect ratio details that the user did not mention. This overrides Pexo's professional judgment.
Calling pexo-chat.sh while the project is in WAIT state. This triggers duplicate video production and wastes credits.
Truncating the video download URL by removing query parameters. This causes a 403 Forbidden error.

faq.question: What is Pexo Agent?
faq.answer: Pexo Agent is a skill that connects to the Pexo AI video platform. You describe the video you want and the skill handles the rest: creating a project, uploading assets, submitting your request, and delivering the final video.

faq.question: What do I need to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need a PEXO_API_KEY from pexo.ai. Set it as an environment variable. You also need curl, jq, and file commands installed on your system.

faq.question: How long does video creation take?
faq.answer: A standard short video takes about 15 to 20 minutes to produce. More complex or longer videos may take additional time.

faq.question: What video formats and aspect ratios are supported?
faq.answer: The skill produces videos in 16:9 (landscape), 9:16 (portrait), and 1:1 (square) aspect ratios. Duration is 5 to 120 seconds.

faq.question: Can I revise a video after it is created?
faq.answer: Yes. Send your feedback to the same project using the revision workflow. Pexo will update the video based on your changes.

faq.question: What happens if I run out of credits?
faq.answer: The skill detects credit errors automatically and provides instructions for purchasing more credits through the Pexo website.
